SYAG vs. Guard Software

Guard software manages your guards. SYAG manages your facility.

Guard management tools are good at what they do: scheduling, GPS tracking, patrol verification, and incident reporting. But they were built for security contracting companies — not for facility operators. And they cover less than 20% of your facility's security signals.

What happens when you only have guard software

You've deployed a guard management tool. Your guards are tracked, patrols are logged, and incidents are filed from mobile. Good.

But:

  • Your cameras don't know your guards exist. Camera events sit in a separate VMS dashboard. When a motion alarm fires, no one is automatically notified. No incident is created. No guard is dispatched.
  • Your IoT devices — panic buttons, sensors, UPS monitors — aren't integrated anywhere. They alert to a separate system, if they alert at all.
  • Your assets are still on a spreadsheet. Your visitor register is still paper. Your incident reports live in a separate system from your patrol logs.

The result: you have operational data in four or five places — no unified picture of your facility.

What guard software covers — and what it doesn't

Capability TrackTik Silvertrac Belfry SYAG
Guard scheduling & GPS ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓
Patrol & checkpoint verification ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓
Mobile incident reporting ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓
Camera / VMS integration ✗ ✗ ✗ ✓
IoT device management ✗ ✗ ✗ ✓
Asset management ✗ ✗ ✗ ✓
Visitor management ✗ ✗ ✗ ✓
Visual flow automation ✗ ✗ ✗ ✓
Multi-tenant SaaS ✓ Partial ✓ ✓
MEA-native, Arabic support ✗ ✗ ✗ ✓

The cost of the gap

When you use guard management software, you still need:

  • A VMS or NVR for cameras: {$500–2,000/month}
  • IoT monitoring software or manual monitoring: {staff time + tools}
  • Asset tracking (spreadsheet or dedicated tool): {staff time}
  • Visitor management (paper register or separate app): {staff time}

Add these up and compare to SYAG's single subscription. The economics change quickly.

Guard software does its job well — for guard companies

TrackTik, Silvertrac, and Belfry are excellent tools for security contracting companies that need to manage large guard workforces across multiple client sites, handle scheduling and payroll, and prove service delivery to clients.

If you are a facility manager — not a security company — your needs are different. You don't need billing, payroll, or client portals. You need a unified operational picture of your facility. That's what SYAG is.

See exactly what SYAG covers that guard software doesn't.
